name: SHM Period and Frequency Calculations
description: Calculate period, frequency, and angular frequency for simple harmonic oscillators including springs and pendulums
identification_signal: Questions asking for period, frequency, or angular frequency of an oscillating system

strategy.step_1: Identify whether the system is a spring-mass or pendulum
strategy.step_3: Convert to frequency (f = 1/T) or angular frequency (ω = 2πf) if needed
strategy.step_4: Check that amplitude does not affect period for ideal SHM

phase_1_recognition.purpose: Identify when to use period/frequency formulas

back.title: Simple Pendulum Period
back.explanation: Use for pendulum where θ < 15°. Period depends only on length L and g, not mass.

back.title: Spring-Mass Period
back.explanation: Use for mass on spring. Period depends on mass m and spring constant k, not amplitude.

spot_it.instructions: Identify which system each problem describes

items.prompt_latex: Block on horizontal frictionless surface attached to spring
Mass swinging at end of light rod

steps.title: Identify System Type
steps.instruction: Look for keywords: spring, pendulum, bob, cord
steps.why: Different formulas apply to different systems

steps.title: Write Period Formula
steps.why: Period is fundamental property of oscillator

checkpoint.question: Does your formula match the system?
checkpoint.answer: Should have m/k for spring, L/g for pendulum

what_mastery_means: Can convert between T, f, and ω fluently

variant_drills.question_variant_1: How does period change if mass is doubled?
variant_drills.question_variant_2: What mass is needed for period of 1 second?
